search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk ng249 falls details emergency department comprehensive assessment
site:who.int ICRC Basic Emergency Care SAMPLE trauma fall assessment
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic trauma diagnosis, imaging or disposition
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: NICE explicitly advises against prediction tools for predicting falls.
Injury diagnosis, testing and disposition remain clinician decisions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: NICE exact evidence covers people 65+ and higher-risk people 50–64; WHO trauma domains broaden mechanism assessment but do not provide a complete pediatric or younger-adult falls pathway.
setting_applicability: NICE explicitly includes emergency departments in community settings; WHO BEC supports emergency trauma assessment.
UAE_applicability: Assessment domains transfer, but UAE geriatric, trauma, safeguarding, imaging, ambulance and referral pathways apply.
recency_verification: NICE NG249 (2025) and WHO/ICRC BEC (2018) exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NICE NG249 is current; WHO/ICRC BEC remains the registered foundational source.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NICE section supports documenting the details, onset or duration and circumstances of a fall in the covered older/higher-risk population; it does not apply a risk score or cover every age.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports asking about the details of a fall when a covered person presents after one and explicitly rejects automatic prediction-tool use.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact comprehensive-assessment section supports documenting functional impact, measured vital data, focused assessment, general appearance and relevant time-critical abnormalities only when clinician-assessed in the covered population.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact WHO/ICRC trauma history supports recording mechanism, assessed injury severity, examination, existing results and clinician-decided disposition for a traumatic fall without diagnosing an injury.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

unresolved_source_gaps: Younger adults and children, exact fall circumstances, syncope, intoxication, anticoagulation, head injury, fracture, safeguarding, examination, imaging, treatment and disposition remain incompletely supported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
